questions.question: 1. A gear is a machine element which is used for transmitting:

explanations.a: Incorrect. Gears transmit power at a constant rate depending on input conditions.
explanations.b: Incorrect. Gears primarily transmit torque, though speed changes as a result of gear ratio.
explanations.c: Correct. Gears are mainly used for transmitting torque between shafts.
explanations.d: Incorrect. One of the given options is correct.

questions.question: 2. Material used for gear extrusion is:

explanations.a: Incorrect. Although steel is commonly used for gears, the extrusion simulation shown uses aluminium.
explanations.b: Correct. Aluminium is used in the extrusion simulation due to its good formability.
explanations.c: Incorrect. In the given context, only aluminium is used.
explanations.d: Incorrect. One of the options is correct.

questions.question: 3. Bevel Gear formation by forging is:

explanations.a: Correct. Bevel gear forging in the shown process is carried out in multiple steps.
explanations.b: Incorrect. It is not completed in a single step.
explanations.c: Incorrect. The shown process specifically follows multiple steps.
explanations.d: Incorrect. One of the options is correct.

questions.question: 4. The static die in the bevel gear forging process is shown in:

explanations.a: Incorrect. Red does not represent the static die.
explanations.b: Correct. The static die is shown in green color in the simulation.
explanations.c: Incorrect. Yellow represents a different component.
explanations.d: Incorrect. One of the options is correct.

questions.question: 5. The movable die in the bevel gear forging process is:

explanations.a: Incorrect. Red does not represent the movable die in the simulation.
explanations.b: Incorrect. Green represents the static die.
explanations.c: Correct. The movable die is shown in yellow color.
explanations.d: Incorrect. One of the options is correct.
